suggested_title: Moon Jeong Lee - Assistant Professor of Ophthalmology, Wilmer Eye Institute
prospect_notes: Dr. Moon Jeong Lee earned her bachelor's degree in biology at Emory University before receiving her MD from Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine (Class of 2020). She completed her ophthalmology residency at the Wilmer Eye Institute, Johns Hopkins Medicine, and a glaucoma fellowship at Wills Eye Hospital, after which she joined the glaucoma division at Wilmer as an assistant professor. She specializes in the medical, laser, and surgical treatment of routine and complex glaucoma, including minimally invasive glaucoma surgeries (MIGS). Her research focuses on aging with vision loss and the impact of physical activity on visual field preservation in glaucoma. She received the EyeCon 2023 inaugural Visionary in Eye Care Resident Recognition Award for Best in Ophthalmology and the ASCRS Resident Excellence Award (2024), among other honors from Wilmer.

_enrichment.title: Assistant Professor of Ophthalmology, Wilmer Eye Institute
